idea.hook: On one planet, a single day lasts LONGER than its entire year!
facts: On Earth, one rotation (a day) takes ~24 hours, and one orbit (a year) takes ~365 days.
Venus rotates incredibly slowly and backwards compared to most planets.
A sidereal day on Venus is 243 Earth days long. Its orbital year is 225 Earth days.
idea.payoff: This means you'd celebrate your birthday on Venus before the sun even finishes crossing the sky once!
idea.title: Venus: A Day Longer Than Its Year! 🤯⏳

scenes.text: ON ONE PLANET, A SINGLE DAY LASTS LONGER THAN ITS ENTIRE YEAR!
scenes.visual: Dramatic shot of Venus, swirling clouds. Text appears boldly.
Split screen: Left shows Earth rotating, right shows Earth orbiting the Sun with clock/calendar icons.

scenes.text: Venus: Rotates incredibly slowly... AND BACKWARDS!
scenes.visual: Close-up of Venus with a slow, counter-clockwise rotation indicated by an arrow. Atmospheric haze.

scenes.text:
Venus Day: 243 Earth Days
Venus Year: 225 Earth Days
scenes.visual: Infographic comparison: A large 'DAY' counter slowly ticking up next to a smaller 'YEAR' counter quickly completing a cycle.

scenes.text: You'd celebrate your birthday on Venus before the sun even finishes crossing the sky ONCE!
scenes.visual: A stylized silhouette of a person on the surface of Venus, looking up at a sun that is barely past the horizon line, emphasizing its slow movement.

youtube.title: Venus: A Day Longer Than Its Year! 🤯⏳
